question: Read: 'At the turn of the 20th century, bottles were found which had been sent by people who were travelling on board the Titanic.' — When were the Titanic bottles found?
choices: At the turn of the 20th century

question: They __________ friends since they were children — over twenty years now.
Read: 'Consequently, the government plans to set up regulations to address these dangers.' — The word 'consequently' is used to show:
Which phrase shows that you feel RELIEVED about something?
choices: I can breathe a sigh of relief now.
I've had it up to here.
You've got to be kidding me!

question: I'll write it down so you can __________ it on to her office later.
Read: 'AI search engines do not always use reliable sources and so information provided by AI might be incomplete or even wrong.' — The word 'reliable' means:
The mother was absolutely __________ when she found out her son had lied to her.
He's __________ that someone will find out his secret one day.
Read: 'Without a doubt there are some challenges, but there are also examples of when the Internet has changed someone's life for the better.' — What does this sentence show?
choices: The author presents both problems and benefits of the Internet

question: By 2000, many people __________ computers in their daily lives.
Read: 'the government plans to set up regulations to address these dangers and strictly control how it is used.' — What does the government plan to do about AI?
choices: Set up regulations to control its use

question: She didn't __________ the new city at first, but grew to love it eventually.
The satellite dish couldn't __________ any signal during the heavy storm.
The teacher __________ three errors in her experiment report very clearly.
She __________ her essay twice before she was happy with it.
Read: 'Although it might make many aspects of life easier, it could also make things worse.' — The word 'although' is used to show:
Enas __________ the windows before the sandstorm started to blow.
Read: 'She saw that different groups of chimpanzees had battles in which they tried to wipe out the rival group.' — The phrasal verb 'wipe out' means:
I __________ my homework before I went to bed last night.
Read: 'She went with her mother to the shores of Lake Tanganyika in Tanzania to start her new job.' — Where did Jane start her new job?
As Omar __________ home last night, someone stole his bicycle.
I don't want to lose __________ with my old friends after we graduate.
Even after fifty years apart, the two old friends managed to __________ touch.
She __________ in a flat in Amman temporarily while she looks for a permanent home.
He __________ that I had made some basic spelling mistakes in my essay.
I __________ Malek every day, but I can't get through — his phone must be off.
This is a very difficult question for everyone, __________?

Read: 'her studies showed her that the chimpanzees could clearly communicate, allowing them to groom and help each other, share food as well as have arguments.' — Name TWO things chimpanzee communication allows them to do.
choices: Speak human language and make tools
Groom each other and share food

question: He __________ the bill when he received a payment reminder.
Internet __________ changed the way people communicate forever.
Be careful not to __________ as rude when you correct someone's mistake.
Which phrase would you use to express that you feel SAD?
choices: You've got to be kidding me!
I can breathe a sigh of relief.
I'm feeling a bit down in the dumps.

question: The chairperson spoke non-stop, so the audience couldn't __________ edgeways.
A: 'I met a really interesting person at the conference.' B: '__________ ? Tell me more.'
Read: 'Children themselves might find they are relying on a form of technology that in its present form should not always be trusted.' — What is the writer warning about?
choices: Children should not use computers at all
Children may depend on untrustworthy technology

question: I am going to pass the exam this time, __________?
I borrowed four books the last time I __________ to the library.
You will be on time for the presentation, __________?
Read: 'Perhaps this is what inspired her to go to Africa.' — The word 'Perhaps' indicates:
The two countries agreed to __________ regular contact through their embassies.
